nodes.context: Outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1967 in Calgary, Alberta, active until 1997 when it patched over to Hells Angels.

nodes.original_text_name: US-based motorcycle club of the same name
nodes.context: US-based club founded in 1965 in Louisville, Kentucky, apparently independent of the Canadian club.
One of four dominant outlaw motorcycle clubs in Alberta prior to 1997.
One of four dominant outlaw motorcycle clubs in Alberta prior to 1997.
One of four dominant outlaw motorcycle clubs in Alberta prior to 1997.
International outlaw motorcycle club; the Grim Reapers patched over to become part of Hells Angels in 1997.
Motorcycle club whose president Ronald Hartley was murdered by Grim Reapers members in 1970.
Outlaw motorcycle club involved in escalating violence in eastern Canada with Hells Angels in 1990s.

edges.context: The Canadian club was apparently independent of the US club of the same name.
In 1997, the Grim Reapers became part of the Hells Angels in a patch-over ceremony.
Grim Reapers members murdered Outcasts president Ronald Hartley in 1970.
Escalating violence between Rock Machine and Hells Angels' Quebec chapters led to Canadian anti-gang legislation.
